{{Quote|This setup is unorthodox and unexpected and can provide you with a tactical edge…if you've got credits to burn.|Gunman|<ref name="GFT" />}}
'''Gunman''' was the alias of a member of [[Churhee's Riflemen]] and consequently of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. Primarily a combat technician, he often made unorthodox use of [[Droid/Legends|droids]] and weaponry to ensure his unit's survival and to outsmart his opponents. He was capable of modifying a broad variety of droids, from [[Medical droid/Legends|medical]] to culinary to [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech units]]. He fought in a number of battles against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] on worlds such as [[Gosho IV]] and [[Yitabo/Legends|Yitabo]], as well as combating [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]] executives, the [[Ailon/Legends|Ailon]] [[Ailon Nova Guard/Legends|Nova Guardsmen]] and other paramilitary organizations.

Gunman frequented newsboards such as [[Cynabar]]'s [[Cynabar's Droid Datalog|Droid Datalog]] and [[Gundark (arms dealer)|Gundark's]] [[Gundark's Gear Datalog|Gear Datalog]] during the early years of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], often sharing anecdotes from his combat experiences.

==Biography==
===Technical ingenuity===
{{Quote|Remember: any tool can give you an advantage if used properly.|Gunman|<ref name="GFT" />}}
Gunman was the alias of a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] who served with [[Churhee's Riflemen]], a group which specialized in "low profile" missions in the [[Sarin sector/Legends|Sarin]] and [[Parmel sector/Legends|Parmel sectors]]. Given his membership in this group, it is very likely Gunman was a highly skilled marksman; all of [[Vlaydm Churhee|Churhee's]] squads were sharpshooters.<ref name="GG9">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]''</ref> However, his primary role was as a combat technician. His first assignment with the Riflemen was against an executive from the [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]] who was involved with a [[Slavery/Legends|slaving]] ring. The executive was protected by formidable defenses, and his proximity to civilian populations further complicated the mission. However, Gunman came up with an ingenious solution: he reprogrammed the executive's [[434-FPC Personal Chef Droid|culinary droid]] to drop the defense net, and the squad was able to capture the entire estate without firing a single shot.<ref name="SWAJ14">[[Star Wars Adventure Journal 14|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 14]]</ref>

Gunman brought this creative ability to all his work for the Riflemen. Another of his early assignments was to maintain [[GHT-series Medevac unit|GHT medevac droids]]; specialist droids used to support battlefield [[Medic/Legends|medics]] and retrieve wounded soldiers from battlefields. Gunman modified his droids to overcome their major weakness; by installing an internal automap in the droids, they were able to track down the wounded without the exact coordinates of the soldier needing to be programmed in. Modifications such as this were typical of Gunman's work, suggestions he gladly passed on over [[Cynabar]]'s [[Cynabar's Droid Datalog|Droid Datalog]] during his later years.<ref name="CFT" />

[[File:ChurheesRiflemen-GG9.jpg|thumb|right|An alien member of Churhee's Riflemen.<ref name="Image">[[West End Games]] artist [[Mike Vilardi]] drew this picture, as well as another depicting a member of the same species captured by a slaver ([[:File:EnslaverWieldingRifle.jpg|Here]]). It is possible this individual is intended to be Gunman; he is one of the few Riflemen named, and the only known to have been captured by slavers.</ref> ]]
Another unconventional use of droids by Gunman's unit was their use of [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droids]]. These they fitted with [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] boosters and a number of other modifications, before dispatching them over the battlefield. The droids were then able to send back a hi-res three-dimensional map of the surrounding terrain, which the Riflemen used to tactical advantage. This technique often saw droid losses of up to 80% but was nevertheless valuable.<ref name="CFT" />

On more urban missions, Gunman used similar modifications to give his team an edge. These included modifying the ubiquitous [[Cam droid/Legends|Hover-Cams]] with espionage programming for reconnaissance, and using [[Jammer/Legends|Jammer]] Packs to isolate his opponents and throw them into disarray. Another trick was to use [[UniTech]] [[Diagnostic Systems Diverter|"Patches"]] (designed to make faults in a system undetectable) in reverse; tricking systems into picking up non-existent faults, thereby setting off alarms and other diversions.<ref name="GFT" />

Gunman was also familiar with [[ASP-7 labor droid/Legends|ASP-7]] droids manufactured by [[Industrial Automaton/Legends|Industrial Automaton]], which the Riflemen often used for weapons maintenance and basic manual labor in combat and [[Stiletto security droid]]s which saw primary use in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="CFT" />

===Combat missions===
{{Quote|They can defend all they want against a frontal assault—they hardly ever expect you to come at them through the walls.|Gunman|<ref name="GFT" />}}
Churhee's Riflemen started out as a mercenary unit primarily contracted to the Galactic Empire. It was highly effective in ripping up the flanks and rear of enemy forces. However, the unit was decimated while on a [[Mission on Y'Trella|mission]] for the Empire in the [[Y'Trella system/Legends|Y'Trella system]], due to inadequate backup. In the months that followed this mission, in which their leader Churhee died, the Riflemen only accepted anti-Imperial missions, and allied itself with the Rebel Alliance.<ref name="AJ">{{JournalCite|15|Special Military Unit Intelligence Update}}</ref> While it was unknown whether Gunman was part of the unit before the loss at Y'Trella, he certainly took part in a number of the anti-Imperial missions following this. On these missions, he applied his talent for the unorthodox to his tactics.<ref name="GFT" />

[[File:Plasticine_Thermite_Gel.jpg|thumb|left|A wad of thermite gel, one of a number of tools favored by Gunman.]]
On one occasion, his unit raided an Imperial weapons dump, acquiring dozens of [[Ubrikkian Industries/Legends|Ubrikkian]] [[R-TechApp Man Trap|man traps]]. A miniaturized reversed repulsorlift field, the man traps were designed to immobilize quarry for capture, however Gunman found a unique application for the devices. While fighting [[Battle of Gosho IV|against]] the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] on [[Gosho IV]], his unit was dug in and set a defensive perimeter using the devices. When the Imperials launched their attack with a salvo of grenades, the man traps were activated, slamming the grenades into the ground where they exploded prematurely. This neutralized the Imperials' initiative, allowing the Riflemen to rally and push them back. Gunman also liked using [[Plasticene Thermite Cube|plasticene thermite gel]] to burn through bunker walls, attacking from unexpected angles.<ref name="GFT" />

During a campaign against an Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] (sometime after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]), Gunman and his team infiltrated his fortress and placed small transceivers near key defenses. Once they retreated undetected, they launched guided missiles using [[PLX-4 Missile Launcher|PLX-4 missile launchers]]. Three dozen missiles streaked in undetected, smashing the enemy's defenses and leaving the way clear for the Riflemen.<ref name="GFT" />

Gunman experienced firsthand Imperial cruelty during a [[Mission to Yitabo|mission]] to release [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] slaves. While doing reconnaissance on a group of Imperial slavers near [[Yitabo/Legends|Yitabo]], Gunman strayed too close and was detected and captured by the slavers. They fitted him with a [[Shock collar/Legends|slaving collar]], capable of delivering high-voltage electricity to the captive. While he described the experience as agony, he nevertheless considered it less painful than that which the slavers endured at the hands of the Wookiees after his unit managed to release them all.<ref name="GFT" />

Gunman also had experience fighting against opponents other than the Empire; he once fought against the distinguished [[Ailon/Legends|Ailon]] [[Ailon Nova Guard/Legends|Nova Guardsmen]] (heirs of a 13,000 year military tradition). The Guardsmen were only at half strength, but managed to fool Gunman's squad into thinking they had twice their actual numbers via [[Holographic Projector Doubler Suit|doubler suits]] (either captured or issued by the Empire). Surviving the encounter, Gunman was impressed by their ingenuity, learning that any tool could confer an advantage in battle if used correctly.<ref name="GFT" />

His old unit had a few production model [[Juggernaut 510 Combat Power Armor]] suits. They were acquired from one of the designers, a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] by the name of [[Sarvel Tal-Oldra]] who set up in [[Klaus Vandangante/Legends|Doc's]] outlaw tech base in the Corporate Sector after the project was canceled. Though he reported the expensive suits as bulky and with a weakness due to its unprotected power transfer routing conduits, he still recommended them. The Riflemen's affiliation to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] also gave Gunman access to [[Nen-Carvon]] [[R-19 Paraglider]]s, developed specially for the [[Alliance Special Forces/Legends|Alliance Special Forces]], which he recommended for covert missions.<ref name="GFT" /> While working for the Alliance, he also helped to swipe a number of [[Seeker Transport|hyperspace pods]] containing [[Seeker AS-M12 message droid|AS-M12-series messenger droids]].<ref name="CFT" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Sounds like your missile "specialist" forgot the first rule of demolitions and explosives delivery: ''clean your weapon''…Always check and recheck your gear.|Gunman|<ref name="GFT" />}}
Gunman was a highly creative individual, finding many unorthodox uses for technology. This led him to test [[CN-15 camouflage netting|camo-netting]] underwater (successfully), and to discover that [[Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc./Legends|Merr-Sonn Weapons]] [[Superior Boarding Armor]] suits could vent their [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]] supply for propulsion if their rocket packs ran out (although venting too much air had its own dangers). Gunman was also familiar with the flaws in technology, such as defective prismatic crystals in Imperial issue [[KK-5 blaster pistol]]s,<ref name="GFT" /> and a minor targeting glitch in [[K4 security droid]]s which threw their targeting sensors and gyros out of sync when moving at speed.<ref name="CFT" />

Gunman willingly shared his insights with others, often giving advice through his alias on various newsnets during the period [[1 BBY/Legends|34:1:7]] to [[10 ABY/Legends|45:3:0]]. While this information was given away freely, Gunman revealed little about himself; his real name and species were never given out, nor were any distinguishing personal features. Considering the Empire was still extant when he posted his comments, this anonymity was likely to ensure he avoided repercussions for his actions.<ref name="GFT" /><ref name="CFT" />

==Behind the scenes==
Gunman first appeared in ''[[Cynabar's Fantastic Technology: Droids]]'' ([[January]] [[1997]]) as one of a number of individuals leaving messages on the datalogs. He was also used in this capacity in ''[[Gundark's Fantastic Technology: Personal Gear]]'' ([[July]] [[1997]]) and in two Droid Datalogs in the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]''. These messages were designed to serve as scenario hooks for gamemasters, or to provide possible modifications for equipment. Others provided explanations for why weapons could fail; gamemasters could draw on these to explain what occurs when a player rolled a "1" or other unlucky number.<ref name="GFT" />

Although around two hundred individuals left messages of this kind in various sourcebooks, Gunman was by far the most prolific, leaving a total of twenty-two messages.
